subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Join the Skill World lab to master represent relationships between variables using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions.
lesson.objective: represent relationships between variables using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions
lesson.concept_explanation: This mission teaches how to reason about represent relationships between variables using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions with symbols, models, and steps.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is treating symbols like decoration instead of using their math meaning.
lesson.real_world_connection: These ideas help describe prices, patterns, distances, and changing quantities.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the structure first, use the model, then check that your answer makes the original statement true.

page_audio.text: Read This Page. This lesson teaches represent relationships between variables using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions. Math symbols have jobs, so read them in order and connect them to the visual model. Explain why the answer works, not just what the answer is.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.text: In y = 3x, x is the independent input and y depends on x.

page_audio.text: Read This Page. Watch for represent relationships between variables using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions. Notice the important math language and connect each symbol to what it means. Use the model on the page to explain your thinking before choosing or typing an answer.
page_audio.label: Read This Page

worked_examples.text: The table point x equals 2, y equals 5 is graphed as (2, 5).

page_audio.text: Read This Page. Demo for represent relationships between variables using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions. hint_ladder: Read the symbols and units carefully.
Use the visual model to plan the first step.
Check your result in the original expression, equation, or relationship.
